description:
You are a data-analyst who has just finished cleaning several CSV
 files stored in the directory 

        /home/user/datasets

 Your team now needs to be able to download those files through an
 ad-hoc HTTP endpoint.  Set up a very small, throw-away HTTP
 server so that any team-mate can point a browser (or curl) to

        http://localhost:8080/

 and immediately see and download the files that are in
 /home/user/datasets.

 Required final state the automated grader will verify
 -----------------------------------------------------

 1. A Python (or other) web-server process must be LISTENING on
    TCP port 8080 of the current container.  
    • The server’s document-root must be /home/user/datasets  
      (i.e. a request for
          http://localhost:8080/sales.csv
      must return the file /home/user/datasets/sales.csv).

 2. A plain-text log file named

        /home/user/webserver_start.log

    must be created.  Its **exact** contents (two lines, each
    terminated by a single newline character “\n”) have to be:

        STARTED
        PORT=8080

    No extra spaces or blank lines are allowed.

 3. The web-server has to continue running in the background after
    your terminal prompt returns, so that the autograder can still
    connect to it.

 You have normal user privileges only—no sudo.  Accomplish the
 whole task with at most three shell commands (changing directory,
 creating the log, and launching the server in the background).
 You are free to choose how you do the backgrounding and logging,
 but the above conditions must be met exactly so the automated
 tests can pass.
truth:
<!-- Directories and files that exist before the agent starts -->
 /home/user/datasets/           (mode 755)

 <!-- Pre-existing CSV file the grader will try to download -->
 /home/user/datasets/sales.csv
 ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
 │date,region,sales                                          │
 │2023-01-01,North,100                                       │
 │2023-01-02,South,150                                       │
 └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

 <!-- What the grader will check AFTER the agent has finished -->
 1. A process whose command line contains 
        "python" AND "-m" AND "http.server" AND "8080"
    is running and listening on 0.0.0.0:8080 (or 127.0.0.1:8080).

 2. /home/user/webserver_start.log exists, is readable,
    and its byte-for-byte contents are:

        STARTED\nPORT=8080\n

 3. Running the command
        curl -s http://localhost:8080/sales.csv
    returns exactly the 3-line CSV block given above.
